reviews.review_msg: Very kind and helpful professor! She's happy to answer any questions, including Pre-Calc basics. The lectures can be a little dry, so sit up front to focus. She's very personal and makes an effort to remember her students' names.

Elena is soft-spoken and caring towards her students, and her exercises can be really beneficial to understand different parts of the calculus topics – even when the topics can get quite tough and context-lacking.
If you get past her initial impression, she's actually great. It's just that the course in itself is pretty difficult. I can see how it may sometimes be hard to understand her, but I got past it.

Elena is amazing when it comes to grading and giving feedback. She's very knowledgeable, but sometimes her voice can make you a bit sleepy. Still, I definitely recommend her—she doesn't deserve all the bad reviews. If you study and work hard, especially using the lecture notes and her exam reviews, you'll do well and get good grades!

Please honestly reflect on how much you've actually studied. Most of the test questions are ones we've already covered in class handout. You can't expect to get good grades without doing your homework. I've seen, it seems like 70% of students want to get good grades without studying even though they have the ability to study.

awful, awful, awful. the worst prof ive ever had. makes everything more difficult for no reason. tests are ridiculous. doesn't teach in class and relies on jamies recorded lectures. strict and unnecessary grader. unreasonable human being. DO NOT TAKE HER!!!
A lot of self learning in this course but tests are based on textbook problems. So if you practice textbook questions you can score good.
Great professor, always willing to help students, and very generous in marking.

Elena is nice! come to her with whenever questions you have and she doesn't mind explaining it over and over. however, tbh, if I hadn't taken Calc in high school, I would have never understood what is going on! She is not a really good explainer. her grading is quite clear. her homework is long but you only need to submit few of the questions.
Elena is very sweet and so are her lectures. The loads of homework you get is for you to get well prepared for the exams. If you do everything she says, you will be able to get good grades.
Nah this teacher sucks.she speaks like a radio hey class pauses right pauses cmon man what the heck
not recommended
Useful handouts, clear instructions, concise and yet effective way of teaching. Do not miss the videos and do a maximum of homework questions. Thank you for the inspiration, Elena!

Great professor. She does not really explain the stuff in class, but honestly i think it's for the best since u can speed up video lectures (plus those lectures are great). She is there to help and explain when you need her. The assignment she gives is comprehensive and she marks it all.

She is the worst teacher one can have for math. Gives long assignments filled with lengthy questions, then asks tough questions on the exam and refuses to elaborate on the criteria based on which she assigns students marks. She blatantly asks us to watch videos of another prof at SFU named Jamie, even during class. Nothing is covered during class.

I took Elena last semester, while she is very kind her lectures are not easy to understand, and you are expected to watch other lecture videos from other professors before the class. She will only give a small summary of the topic and let you work on homeworks or excercises.

She barely teaches anything, you must read the content that she provides or the textbook before coming to class. She will mostly answer doubts and talk about the bits of the content. Until now, classes are basically a mandatory 4-hour "office hour:"
Doesn't teach in person, expects us to watch video lectures that are NOT by her, but rather Khan Academy and other lectures. Heavy assignment workload. Avoid her by all cost

Actually I have basic knowledge of calculus so I didnt watch her videos and only read the textbook myself. However, I think she is quit nice when I joined her office hours and the homework actually is needed for your own practice. The exam questions are reasonable if you done her homework from textbook!

Work hard , listen to all lectures and more importantly do things on time . You will get an A - easily . Elena is so kind , she responds to all your doubts . But the only thing bad about Elena is she doesnt teach us , she tell us to look for DR Jamies videos for the whole semester. This is really annoying. Jamie Mullohands lecture are super.
She does not explain clearly

I am currently taking asynchronous class and prof Elena is the one who is SUPPOSED to teach me but in reality, it's youtube and khan academy that's been helping me, She only put out lectures for like just 2 weeks and those were too short and with bad audio. She just posts links to youtube vids and khan academy. Please never take her async class.

Hi , Dr. Halmaghi was one of the best professor I had in my first semester. She is very kind. At first of her class , the assignments were a bit heavy but as she went further , the assignments got less. The quizzes and final exam's questions are easy if you do all the assignments carefully and absorb her lectures.

Took her online course and I really had the toughest times of my life coping up. No lecture videos, plenty of assignments, no solution to the problems. Instead, she would ask me to watch khan academy and other sources to take help from. I really don't know why would anyone even pay for her course. On the flip side tho, she's a nice human being.

She is a caring instructor but her lectures might be a bit difficult to understand at first as her voice isn't very loud. Although her classes may seem boring, she would help any student if they just ask her questions. She appreciates class participation and if you pay attention to the quizzes and sample questions, you should do well in her exams.
She is really fun as long as you get further learn of her. Trust me.

Extremely slow in class. Can't keep the class going for too long, seems like she needs more break than her students. Moreover, very less overall practise done in class but dose give a lot homework.  She gives way too much background information than required, most of the time not even related to math.
No help whatsoever. Demotivates students who doesn't know how to do problems. Be prepared to self-teach if you're in her class.
not very helpful
First day of class she throws a question for everyone to answer, chooses someone to answer, and then criticize their answer without giving out her own solution. This went on for the rest of the year.
She is nice, but it took me a while to understand her European accent. As long as you make good use of her notes and do some extra practices, you will be fine (A range)

Personally, this was an easy A+. Not so for the general class. You MUST show up for her quizzes and exams - no make-ups permitted. She isn't the easiest to understand (Romanian accent), but treats you well if you try and don't give her lip. Definitely do your online assignments and written homework. Be prepared for lots of word problems.
Take at your own risk.

Do not take this class  unless you're a good self learner. She expects you to know all rules and details of math equations, although many are taking this to upgrade their mark.  I did not find her to be a good professor. All you did in class was go through word problems and none of the weekly material.. would not recommend taking FAN with her.
Nice person. Extremely bad prof. Recommend you not to take.

She's quite rude and expects you to know the material. I asked her what I did wrong in a question and she said everything in a very condescending manner. Her thick accent interrupts is hard to understand which makes it difficult to comprehend. I don't understand how some people find her appealing. Her classes are a basically self-teach class.

If you're not a self taught person be prepared to drop her class. Although she is very nice as a person, as a prof I wouldn't recommend her. Her teaching methods are not clear, and she had a hard time understanding my questions. Her lectures are long and useless (Unless there was a quiz that day) and she drags the class without teaching anything.
Be clear with lectures and homework so that you do the quizzes well. You're through.
Very poor lectrue, you will fall asleep in her class Do not take her course anymore

Elena seems like she's interested in her topic but you can barely understand her. She's also quite rude for no reason. Incl. her thick accent, she gives no clear instruction and kind of drags the class along in the dark. Basically a self-teach class, as the notes are left for you to read online and class "lectures" consist of endless word problems.
She was not at all helpful. She has a really boring voice. One can have a good sleep .

Good prof. She's softspoken so make sure to sit at the front of the class. Good lectures, covers the material cleanly and sticks to the syllabus. Her review sessions are awesome-it's possible to ONLY study the review material and pull an A in the course.

This course requires you to actually do some work. Math is something I've struggled with & actually attending the Q workshops helped out tons, unlike the rest of the complainers who sat in class laughing the entire time & didnt go. No wonder they did poorly. Elena was VERY helpful if you actually pay attention and come to class willing to learn.
Elena has such a calm way about you and I really appreciated her ability to explain things to us in a way that was easily understandable.
Not clear at all. Without friends and youtube would not have passed this class. She was useless.
Explains concepts poorly. I would not recommend at all. I didn't have a good experience in this class what so ever. Also, expect difficult midterms and quizzes/assignments due EACH week.
Great prof.
Not recommended.
Best experience ever
really horrible class! do not take!
